DIA_DIAG_HAE.name: Hereditary Angioedema
DIA_DIAG_HAE.description: Hereditary angioedema is a rare inherited disorder characterized by recurrent episodes of the accumulation of fluids outside of the blood vessels, blocking the normal flow of blood or lymphatic fluid and causing rapid swelling of tissues in the hands, feet, limbs, face, intestinal tract, or airway.

DIA_DIAG_AAE.name: Acquired Angioedema
DIA_DIAG_AAE.description: Acquired angioedema (AAE) is characterized by acquired deficiency of C1 inhibitor (C1-INH), hyperactivation of the classical pathway of human complement and angioedema symptoms mediated by bradykinin released by inappropriate activation of the contact-kinin system.

DIA_DIAG_ACE_INH.name: Ace Inhibitor Angioedema
DIA_DIAG_ACE_INH.description: Angiotensin-converting enzyme (ACE) inhibitors are the leading cause of drug-induced angioedema in the United States because they are so widely prescribed. Patients most commonly present with swelling of the lips, tongue, or face, although another presentation is episodic abdominal pain due to intestinal angioedema.

DIA_DIAG_AIP.name: Acute Intermittent Porphyria
DIA_DIAG_AIP.description: Acute intermittent porphyria is a rare autosomal dominant disease characterized by a deficiency of hydroxymethylbilane synthase (HMBS). It presents with abdominal pain, nausea, vomiting, peripheral neuropathy, and seizures.

DIA_DIAG_NAEGLERISIS.name: Naeglearia Fowleri
DIA_DIAG_NAEGLERISIS.description: Naegleri fowleri is an amoeba that can cause a serious central nervous system infection. The amoeba is found in warm and still fresh water bodies of water and enters a human body through the nose. Successful treatment has included miltefosine.

DIA_DIAG_CLARKSONS.name: Clarksons Disease
DIA_DIAG_CLARKSONS.description: Idiopathic systemic capillary leak syndrome (SCLS), also known as Clarkson's disease and spontaneous periodic edema, is a potentially fatal disorder characterized by stereotypic “attacks”of varying intensity of hypovolemic shock and generalized edema in association with hemoconcentration (as detected by an elevated hematocrit concentration) and hypoalbuminemia, typically occurring in the absence of albuminuria.

DIA_DIAG_WILSONS.name: Wilsons Disease
DIA_DIAG_WILSONS.description: An inherited disorder that causes too much copper to accumulate in the organs. In Wilson's disease, copper isn't eliminated properly and instead accumulates, possibly to a life-threatening level.

DIA_DIAG_COMPARTMENT.name: Compartment Syndrome
DIA_DIAG_COMPARTMENT.description: Compartment syndrome is a painful condition that occurs when pressure within the muscles builds to dangerous levels. This pressure can decrease blood flow, which prevents nourishment and oxygen from reaching nerve and muscle cells.

DIA_DIAG_NPT3.name: Niemann Pick Type 3
DIA_DIAG_NPT3.description: Niemann-Pick disease refers to a group of inherited metabolic disorders in which abnormal amounts of lipids (fatty materials such as waxes, oils, and cholesterol) build up in the brain, spleen, liver, lungs, and bone marrow.

DIA_DIAG_ZES.name: Zollinger-Ellison Syndrome
DIA_DIAG_ZES.description: Zollinger-Ellison syndrome is caused by a gastrinoma, which causes trophic effects of gastrin hypersecreting gastric acid. It can cause severe abdominal pain from multiple ulcers in the esophagus, stomach, and duodenum, diarrhea, and heartburn. PPIs have been the main therapy to control the symptoms.

DIA_DIAG_MENETRIERS.name: Menetriers Disease
DIA_DIAG_MENETRIERS.description: Menetrier disease is a rare disorder characterized by giant mucosal folds in the proximal part of the stomach, diminished acid secretion, and a protein-losing state with hypoalbuminemia. Other names for Menetrier disease are hypoproteinemic hypertrophic gastropathy and giant hypertrophic gastritis.

DIA_DIAG_ACHALASIA.name: Achalasia
DIA_DIAG_ACHALASIA.description: Achalasia is a rare swallowing disorder that affects the esophagus (the tube between the throat and the stomach). In people with achalasia, the esophagus muscles do not contract properly and do not help propel food down toward the stomach.

DIA_DIAG_MALS.name: Median Arcuate Ligament Syndrome
DIA_DIAG_MALS.description: Median arcuate ligament syndrome (MALS) also called as Dunbar syndrome is a vascular compression syndrome. It is technically known as celiac artery compression syndrome resulting from the compression of celiac axis by median arcuate ligament and diaphragmatic crura.

DIA_DIAG_BOUVERET.name: Bouveret Syndrome
DIA_DIAG_BOUVERET.description: Bouveret syndrome is a rare form of gallstone ileus secondary to an acquired fistula between the gallbladder and either the duodenum or stomach. Through the fistula, a gallstone may enter the enteric system and cause a gastric outlet obstruction.

DIA_DIAG_POTS.name: Postural Orthostatic Tachycardia Syndrome
DIA_DIAG_POTS.description: Postural orthostatic tachycardia syndrome (POTS) is one of a group of disorders that have orthostatic intolerance (OI) as their primary symptom. OI is a condition in which an excessively reduced volume of blood returns to the heart after an individual stands up from a lying down position.

DIA_DIAG_BCS.name: Budd-Chiari Syndrome
DIA_DIAG_BCS.description: Budd-Chiari syndrome (BCS) is an uncommon disorder characterized by obstruction of hepatic venous outflow. The obstruction may be thrombotic or non-thrombotic anywhere along the venous course from the hepatic venules to junction of the inferior vena cava (IVC) to the right atrium.

DIA_DIAG_BSS.name: Bernard-Soulier Syndrome
DIA_DIAG_BSS.description: Bernard-Soulier syndrome is a rare inherited blood clotting disorder that is characterized by unusually giant platelet cells, thrombocytopenia, and prolonged bleeding time. Affected individuals tend to bleed excessively and incur spontaneous ecchymoses.

DIA_DIAG_EVANS.name: Evans Syndrome
DIA_DIAG_EVANS.description: Evans syndrome is an autoimmune condition that presents with two or more cytopenias, which commonly includes autoimmune hemolytic anemia (AIHA) and immune thrombocytopenia (ITP), with or without immune neutropenia. It can be primary (or idiopathic) or secondary (i.e., associated with an underlying disorder).

DIA_DIAG_ADEM.name: Acute Disseminated Encephalomyelitis
DIA_DIAG_ADEM.description: Acute disseminated encephalomyelitis (ADEM) involves a brief but intense attack of inflammation (swelling) in the brain and spinal cord that damages the brain's myelin. Myelin is the protective covering of nerve fibers. Myelin is also called white matter due to its color.

DIA_DIAG_ARPKD.name: Autosomal Recessive Polycystic Kidney Disease
DIA_DIAG_ARPKD.description: Autosomal recessive polycystic kidney disease (ARPKD) is the recessive form of polycystic kidney disease. It is associated with a group of congenital fibrocystic syndromes.

DIA_DIAG_BEHCETS.name: Behcets Disease
DIA_DIAG_BEHCETS.description: Behcet's disease is a rare disease that causes chronic inflammation, or swelling, in the body's blood vessels. Behcet's disease can affect many parts of the body, including the brain and spinal cord.

DIA_DIAG_BIOTIN.name: Biotinidase Deficiency
DIA_DIAG_BIOTIN.description: Biotinidase deficiency is an autosomal recessive metabolic disorder in which biotin is not released from proteins in the diet during digestion or from normal protein turnover in the cell. This situation results in biotin deficiency.

DIA_DIAG_ABDOEPILEPSY.name: Abdominal Epilepsy
DIA_DIAG_ABDOEPILEPSY.description: Abdominal epilepsy is a rare condition most frequently found in children, consisting of gastrointestinal disturbances caused by epileptiform seizure activity. Though a few cases of it have been reported in adults too.

DIA_DIAG_ABDOMIGRAINE.name: Abdominal Migraine
DIA_DIAG_ABDOMIGRAINE.description: Abdominal migraine (AM) is a functional disorder that usually manifests in childhood and adolescence, without a clear pathologic mechanism or biochemical irregularity. Children frequently experience sporadic episodes of excruciating central abdominal pain accompanied by migrainous symptoms like nausea, vomiting, severe headaches, and general pallor. Abdominal migraine can be diagnosed based off clinical criteria and the exclusion of other disorders.

DIA_DIAG_DJS.name: Dubin-Johnson Syndrome
DIA_DIAG_DJS.description: Dubin–Johnson syndrome is a rare, autosomal recessive, benign disorder that causes an isolated increase of conjugated bilirubin in the serum. Classically, the condition causes a black liver due to the deposition of a pigment similar to melanin. This pigment is due to polymerized epinephrine metabolites, not bilirubin.

DIA_DIAG_BDBS.name: Wyburn-Mason Syndrome
DIA_DIAG_BDBS.description: Bonnet–Dechaume–Blanc syndrome, also known as Wyburn-Mason syndrome, is a rare congenital disorder characterized by arteriovenous malformations of the brain, retina or facial nevi. The syndrome has a number of possible symptoms and can, more rarely, affect the skin, bones, kidneys, muscles, and gastrointestinal tract. When the syndrome affects the brain, people can experience severe headaches, seizures, acute stroke, meningism, and progressive neurological deficits due to acute or chronic ischaemia caused by arteriovenous shunting.

DIA_DIAG_UPTE.name: Unilateral Post-Traumatic Epilepsy
DIA_DIAG_UPTE.description: Post-traumatic epilepsy (PTE) is a form of acquired epilepsy that results from brain damage caused by physical trauma to the brain (traumatic brain injury, abbreviated TBI). A person with PTE experiences repeated post-traumatic seizures (PTS, seizures that result from TBI) more than a week after the initial injury.

SYM_CRP_SEIZURES.name: Seizures
SYM_CRP_SEIZURES.description: A seizure is a sudden, uncontrolled electrical disturbance in the brain. It can cause changes in your behaviour, movements or feelings, and in levels of consciousness.

SYM_CRP_ULCERATION_GENITAL.name: Genital Ulcer
SYM_CRP_ULCERATION_GENITAL.description: Genital ulcers, defined as single or multiple vesicular, ulcerative or erosive lesions of the genital tract, with or without inguinal lymphadenopathy, should lead to consideration of sexually transmitted infection.

SYM_CRP_APTHOUS_ULCERS.name: Apthous Ulcers
SYM_CRP_APTHOUS_ULCERS.description: Oral examination has revealed the presence of apthous ulcers. These are recurrent round or oval sores inside the mouth on an area where the skin is not tightly bound to the underlying bone, such as on the inside of the lips and cheeks or underneath the tongue.

SYM_OL_LIP_EDEMA.name: Lip Angioedema
SYM_OL_LIP_EDEMA.description: The patient has swollen lips due to Angioedema.

SYM_OL_LARYNGEAL_EDEMA.name: Laryngeal Angioedema
SYM_OL_LARYNGEAL_EDEMA.description: The patient has Angioedema in their Larynx leading to difficulty breathing.

SYM_OL_INTESTINAL_EDEMA.name: Intestinal Angioedema
SYM_OL_INTESTINAL_EDEMA.description: The patient has Angioedema in their Intestines causing intense abdomen pain.

SYM_OL_KFR.name: Kayser-Fleischer Rings
SYM_OL_KFR.description: Kayser-Fleischer rings are dark rings that appear to encircle the cornea of the eye. They are due to copper deposition in the Descemet's membrane as a result of particular liver diseases.

SYM_OL_SPLENOMEGALY.name: Splenomegaly
SYM_OL_SPLENOMEGALY.description: An enlarged spleen is also known as splenomegaly.

SYM_OL_RUP_SPLENOMEGALY.name: Ruptured Spleen
SYM_OL_RUP_SPLENOMEGALY.description: A ruptured spleen is an emergency medical condition that occurs when the capsule-like covering of the spleen breaks open, pouring blood into your abdominal area. Depending on the size of the rupture, a large amount of internal bleeding can occur.

SYM_OL_HEPATOMEGALY.name: Hepatomegaly
SYM_OL_HEPATOMEGALY.description: Hepatomegaly is enlargement of the liver beyond its normal size.

SYM_OL_RENAL_FAILURE.name: Renal Failure
SYM_OL_RENAL_FAILURE.description: Renal Failure means one or both of your kidneys no longer function well on their own.

SYM_OL_PULSELESS.name: Pulseless
SYM_OL_PULSELESS.description: Absent pulse is a late sign and indicates tissue death.

SYM_OL_KIDNEY_CYSTS.name: Kidney Cysts
SYM_OL_KIDNEY_CYSTS.description: Imaging has revealed multiple cysts on the patients kidneys.

SYM_OL_DEATH.name: Imminent Death
SYM_OL_DEATH.description: The patient is untreatable as their disease has progressed too far.

SYM_DIAG_C1INH.name: Abnormal C1 Esterase Inhibitor
SYM_DIAG_C1INH.description: C1 inhibitor levels are reduced in cases of hereditary angioedema. In this condition, 85% of patients will have decreased antigenic and functional C1 inhibitor levels, whereas 15% will have a dysfunctional protein.

SYM_DIAG_C1Q.name: Abnormal C1Q and C1 Esterase Inhibitor
SYM_DIAG_C1Q.description: Blood test showed low C1Q and low or dysfunctional C1 Inhibitor.

SYM_DIAG_ACE_INH.name: ACE Inhibitor Detected in Blood Test
SYM_DIAG_ACE_INH.description: Blood test has revealed ACE Inhibitors in the blood and a normal C1 Inhibitor level.

SYM_DIAG_AIP.name: Abnormal PBG in Urine
SYM_DIAG_AIP.description: A raised urine porphobilinogen (PBG) is suggestive of an acute attack of porphyria.

SYM_DIAG_NAEGLERISIS.name: Naeglearia Fowleri Detected
SYM_DIAG_NAEGLERISIS.description: CSF Analysis has found the free-living amoeba Naegleria Fowleri causing Meningoencephalitis.

SYM_DIAG_CLARKSONS.name: Capillary Leakage Findings
SYM_DIAG_CLARKSONS.description: Evaluation of symtoms including Arterial hypotension, hemoconcentration and hypoalbuminemia in absence of secondary causes of shock and infection indicates Clarksons Disease (Idiopathic Capillary Leak Syndrome).

SYM_DIAG_WILSONS.name: Wilsons Disease Findings
SYM_DIAG_WILSONS.description: Liver biopsy showing more than 250 ug/g of dried liver tissue confirms wilsons disease.

SYM_DIAG_COMPARTMENT.name: High Muscle Compartment Pressure
SYM_DIAG_COMPARTMENT.description: Manometer readings of >30 mmHg in the muscle compartment is consistant with compartment syndrome and is at risk of ischemic necrosis.

SYM_DIAG_NPT3.name: Gene Identified
SYM_DIAG_NPT3.description: Genetic testing reveals a mutation in the npc1/npc2 gene.

SYM_DIAG_ZES.name: Multiple Gastrinoma
SYM_DIAG_ZES.description: MRI has revealed multiple gastrinoma in the pancreas

SYM_DIAG_MENETRIERS.name: Menetriers Biopsy Findings
SYM_DIAG_MENETRIERS.description: Mucosal biopsy taken during endoscopy is consistant with menetriers disease.

SYM_DIAG_ACHALASIA.name: Abnormal Esophageal Motility
SYM_DIAG_ACHALASIA.description: Esophageal manometry shows abnormal motility consistant with achalasia.

SYM_DIAG_MALS.name: MALS Findings in Echocardiograph
SYM_DIAG_MALS.description: Doppler ultrasonography measuring peak systolic velocities greater than 200cm/s in the celiac artery are suggestive of celiac artery stenosis associated with MALS.

SYM_DIAG_BOUVERET.name: Bouveret Syndrome Findings on CT
SYM_DIAG_BOUVERET.description: CT scan has revealed presence of a bilioduodenal fistula that has gastric outlet obstruction.

SYM_DIAG_POTS.name: Excessive Heart Rate Increase
SYM_DIAG_POTS.description: An active stand test found an increase in heart rate greater than 30 beats per minute.

SYM_DIAG_BCS.name: Abnormal Liver Ultrasound
SYM_DIAG_BCS.description: Liver ultrasound reveals significant damage to hepatic veins with signs of thrombosis and/or stenosis.

SYM_DIAG_BSS.name: Gene Identified
SYM_DIAG_BSS.description: Genetic testing has revealed abnormalities in GP1BA, GP1BB and GP9 genes.

SYM_DIAG_EVANS.name: Evans Syndrome Findings
SYM_DIAG_EVANS.description: CBC testing revealed the presence of both hemolytic anemia and immune thrombocytopenic purpura with a positive coombs test suggestive of Evans syndrome.

SYM_DIAG_ADEM.name: ADEM Findings on MRI
SYM_DIAG_ADEM.description: MRI revealed multifocal demyelinating lesions in the brain suggestive of ADEM.

SYM_DIAG_ARPKD.name: Gene Identified
SYM_DIAG_ARPKD.description: Genetic testing reveals abnormalities in the PKHD1 gene.

SYM_DIAG_BEHCETS.name: Rucurrent Ulcers
SYM_DIAG_BEHCETS.description: Clinical evaluation of recurrent ulcers on mouth and genitals along with an elevated crp suggests behcets disease.

SYM_DIAG_BIOTIN.name: Gene Identified
SYM_DIAG_BIOTIN.description: Genetic testing revealed abnormalities in the BTD gene.

SYM_DIAG_ABDOEPILEPSY.name: Abdominal Epilepsy Findings
SYM_DIAG_ABDOEPILEPSY.description: Abnormal EEG shows characteristics of Abdominal Epilepsy.

SYM_DIAG_ABDOMIGRAINE.name: Signs of Abdominal Migraines
SYM_DIAG_ABDOMIGRAINE.description: Diagnostic evaluation of patients symptoms and exclusion of other diagnoses indicates a diagnosis of Abdominal Migraines.

SYM_DIAG_DJS.name: Dubin-Johnson Syndrome Findings
SYM_DIAG_DJS.description: In patients with Dubin–Johnson syndrome coproporphyrin I is 3–4 times higher than coproporphyrin III. Analysis of urine porphyrins shows a normal level of coproporphyrin, but the I isomer accounts for 80% of the total (normally 25%).

SYM_DIAG_BDBS.name: Wyburn-Mason Syndrome Findings
SYM_DIAG_BDBS.description: Fluorescent Angiogram has revealed AVMs (Arteriovenous Malformations) in the brain and retina.

SYM_DIAG_UPTE.name: Epileptic TBI Findings
SYM_DIAG_UPTE.description: MRI Brain scan has findings consistant with a TBI causing epilepsy.

EXM_CRP_PELVIC_EXAM.name: Pelvic Exam
EXM_CRP_PELVIC_EXAM.description: A pelvic exam is a doctor's visual and physical examination of a woman's reproductive organs. During the exam, the doctor inspects the vagina, cervix, fallopian tubes, vulva, ovaries, and uterus.

EXM_CRP_FISH_SAMPLE.name: F.I.S.H. Sampling
EXM_CRP_FISH_SAMPLE.description: Fluorescence in situ hybridization (FISH) is a molecular cytogenetic technique that uses fluorescent probes that bind to only those parts of a nucleic acid sequence with a high degree of sequence complementarity. It was developed by biomedical researchers in the early 1980s to detect and localize the presence or absence of specific DNA sequences on chromosomes. Fluorescence microscopy can be used to find out where the fluorescent probe is bound to the chromosomes. FISH is often used for finding specific features in DNA for use in genetic counseling, medicine, and species identification. FISH can also be used to detect and localize specific RNA targets (mRNA, lncRNA and miRNA) in cells, circulating tumor cells, and tissue samples. \n\nRequired Lab: Histology

EXM_OL_C1_BLOOD_SAMPLE.name: C1 Blood Sampling
EXM_OL_C1_BLOOD_SAMPLE.description: C1-inhibitor is a protease inhibitor belonging to the serpin superfamily. Its main function is the inhibition of the complement system to prevent spontaneous activation but also as the major regulator of the contact system. \n\nRequired Lab: Hematology

EXM_OL_C1_BLOOD_TEST.name: C1 Blood Testing
EXM_OL_C1_BLOOD_TEST.description: Hematology technicians are currently working with the sample provided by the patient. They should return their results soon.

EXM_OL_PORPHYRIA_URINE_SAMPLE.name: Porphyria Urine Sampling
EXM_OL_PORPHYRIA_URINE_SAMPLE.description: A raised urine PBG is the diagnostic hallmark of an acute attack of porphyria. \n\nRequired Lab: Histology

EXM_OL_PORPHYRIA_URINE_TEST.name: Porphyria Urine Testing
EXM_OL_PORPHYRIA_URINE_TEST.description: Microbiology technicians are currently working with the sample provided by the patient. They should return their results soon.

EXM_OL_MANOMETER.name: Manometer
EXM_OL_MANOMETER.description: Compartment pressures are often measured with a manometer, a device that detects intracompartmental pressure by measuring the resistance that is present when a saline solution is injected into the compartment. \n\nRequired room: Any ward / unit

EXM_OL_ESOPHAGEAL_MANOMETER.name: Esophageal Manometer
EXM_OL_ESOPHAGEAL_MANOMETER.description: This test involves placing a thin, pressure-sensitive tube through your nose into your esophagus to measure pressure as you swallow. \n\nRequired room: Any diagnostic unit

TRT_STANOZOLOL.name: Stanozolol
TRT_STANOZOLOL.description: Stanozolol may be used in the long-term treatment of patients with hereditary angioedema provided such patients are closely supervised with routine clinical, biochemical, and radiologic assessments. \n\nRequired room: Any office

TRT_MILTEFOSINE.name: Miltefosine
TRT_MILTEFOSINE.description: Miltefosine, sold under the trade name Impavido among others, is a medication mainly used to treat leishmaniasis and free-living amoeba infections such as Naegleria fowleri and Balamuthia mandrillaris. \n\nRequired room: Any office

TRT_ZAVESCA.name: Zavesca
TRT_ZAVESCA.description: Zavesca is a medicine that is used to treat mild to moderate type 1 Gaucher disease in adults and it is also used to treat Niemann-Pick type C. \n\nRequired room: Any office

TRT_BIOTIN.name: Biotin Capsules
TRT_BIOTIN.description: Biotin (vitamin B7) is a vitamin found in foods like eggs, milk, and bananas. Oral Biotin is offered as an over the counter supplement and is used in the treatment of Biotin deficiency. \n\nRequired room: Any office

TRT_INTRAVENOUS_BERINERT.name: Berinert
TRT_INTRAVENOUS_BERINERT.description: Intravenous (IV) Infusion of Berinert(C1-INH concentrate). \n\nRequired room: Any ward / unit

TRT_INTRAVENOUS_PANHEMATIN.name: Panhematin
TRT_INTRAVENOUS_PANHEMATIN.description: Panhematin is a hemin for injection prescription medication used to relieve repeated attacks of acute intermittent porphyria. \n\nRequired room: Any ward / unit

SUR_OL_DISTAL_PANCREATECTOMY.name: Distal Pancreatectomy
SUR_OL_DISTAL_PANCREATECTOMY.description:
Distal pancreatectomy is a surgery to remove the distal (body-tail) part of
				your pancreas. \n\nRequired room: Operating room at Department of Diagnostics.

SUR_OL_FASCIOTOMY.name: Fasciotomy
SUR_OL_FASCIOTOMY.description: Fasciotomy, a procedure in which the fascia is cut to relieve pressure in the muscle compartment, is used to treat people with acute or chronic compartment syndrome. \n\nRequired room: Operating room at Department of Diagnostics.

SUR_OL_GASTRECTOMY.name: Gastrectomy
SUR_OL_GASTRECTOMY.description: A gastrectomy is a medical procedure where all or part of the stomach is surgically removed. \n\nRequired room: Operating room at Department of Diagnostics.

SUR_OL_LIVER_TRANSPLANT.name: Liver Transplant
SUR_OL_LIVER_TRANSPLANT.description: A liver transplant is surgery to replace a diseased liver with a healthy liver from another person. A whole liver may be transplanted, or just part of one. \n\nRequired room: Operating room at Department of Diagnostics.

SUR_OL_MYOTOMY.name: Myotomy
SUR_OL_MYOTOMY.description: Heller myotomy is a surgical procedure in which the muscles of the cardia are cut, allowing food and liquids to pass to the stomach. \n\nRequired room: Operating room at Department of Diagnostics.

SUR_OL_LAPAROSCOPY.name: Laparoscopic Surgery
SUR_OL_LAPAROSCOPY.description: A minimally invasive surgical technique. The surgery is performed by cutting a series of small incisions through which surgical equipment is inserted. \n\nRequired room: Operating room at Department of Diagnostics

SUR_OL_SPLENECTOMY.name: Splenectomy
SUR_OL_SPLENECTOMY.description: Splenectomy is a surgical procedure to remove your spleen. The spleen is an organ that sits under your rib cage on the upper left side of your abdomen. It helps fight infection and filters unneeded material, such as old or damaged blood cells, from your blood. The most common reason for splenectomy is to treat a ruptured spleen, which is often caused by an abdominal injury. Splenectomy may be used to treat other conditions, including an enlarged spleen that is causing discomfort (splenomegaly), some blood disorders, certain cancers, infection, and noncancerous cysts or tumors. \n\nRequired room: Operating room at Department of Diagnostics

SUR_OL_AVM_RESECTION.name: AVM Resection
SUR_OL_AVM_RESECTION.description: AVM resection involves delicately separating the AVM from the surrounding tissue. Using a microscope, a neurosurgeon cuts off the blood supply to the AVM and removes it. \n\nRequired room: Operating room at Neurology dpt

SUR_OL_HEMISPHERECTOMY.name: Anatomical Hemispherectomy
SUR_OL_HEMISPHERECTOMY.description: An anatomic hemispherectomy involves physically removing the diseased hemisphere of the brain including the frontal, parietal, temporal and occipital lobes. The deeper structures in the brain including the thalamus and basal ganglia are left in place. \n\nRequired room: Operating room at Neurology dpt

EXM_SPINAL_FLUID_ANALYSIS_SAMPLING.name: CSF Sampling
EXM_SPINAL_FLUID_ANALYSIS_SAMPLING.description:
Sampling of cerebrospinal fluid is performed by inserting a needle through the lower back into the spinal canal, draining a small amount of the fluid. 

Required room: Histology Lab 

Sampling: Neurology dpt / DID / Department of Diagnostics 

Required equipment: Glassware + Medical centrifuge + Lab analyzer

EXM_LARYNGOSCOPY.name: Laryngoscopy
EXM_LARYNGOSCOPY.description: A procedure where a flexible tube (endoscope) with a tiny video camera and a light source in the end is inserted through the nose to examine the larynx, glottis, and vocal chords. Laryngoscopy is also used to facilitate tracheal intubation. \n\nRequired room: Doctor's office / Observation room / Trauma center / ICU / Department of Diagnostics

EXM_OPHTHALMOSCOPY.name: Ophthalmoscopy
EXM_OPHTHALMOSCOPY.description: A procedure where the fundus/retina is visualised through a funduscope/opthalmoscope. \n\nRequired room: Doctor's office / Observation room / Trauma center / ICU / Office/Diagnostic unit at Emergency / ICU / Internal Medicine dpt / Neurology dpt / Department of Diagnostics

EXM_GASTROSCOPY.name: Gastroscopy
EXM_GASTROSCOPY.description: A procedure where a flexible tube (endoscope) with a tiny video camera and a light source in the end is inserted through the mouth in order to examine the upper part of digestive tract.\n\nRequired room: ICU / Office / Diagnostic unit at General Surgery dpt / Internal Medicine dpt / Cardiology dpt / Department of Diagnostics

EXM_AUDIOMETRY.name: Audiometry
EXM_AUDIOMETRY.description: Audiometric testing determines the patient's level of hearing. \n\nRequired room: Doctor's office / Observation room / ICU / Office / Diagnostic unit at Emergency / ICU / General Surgery dpt / Internal Medicine dpt / Department of Diagnostics

TRT_CLOT_PREVENTING_DRUGS.name: Anticoagulants
TRT_CLOT_PREVENTING_DRUGS.description: Drugs preventing blood platelets to stick to each other and forming clots. \n\nRequired room: ICU / Office / Diagnostic unit at Cardiology dpt / General Surgery dpt / Department of Diagnostics

TRT_INTRAVENOUS_IMMUNOGLOBULIN.name: IV Immunoglobulin
TRT_INTRAVENOUS_IMMUNOGLOBULIN.description: A purified blood product containing antibodies delivered by the intravenous (IV) route. \n\nRequired room: ICU / Office/Diagnostic unit at Orthopaedy dpt / Neurology dpt / Department of Diagnostics

TRT_DIURETICS.name: Diuretics
TRT_DIURETICS.description: Medications that lower the amount of sodium and water in the body. \n\nRequired room: ICU / Office/Diagnostic unit at General Surgery dpt / Internal Medicine dpt / Cardiology / Neurology / Department of Diagnostics

TRT_DEBRIDEMENT.name: Debridement
TRT_DEBRIDEMENT.description: Medical removal of death tissue. \n\nRequired room: Office/Diagnostic unit / Burn unit at Traumatology dpt. / ICU / Trauma center at Emergency / Department of Diagnostics
